LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARLSON BOULEVARD LP DBA PULLMAN POINT MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE DESPICABLE AND WILLFUL CONDUCT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ALLEGED ANY INTENTIONAL TORTS, OR THAT PULLMAN WAS SPECIALLY AWARE OF THE ROOFERS' CONDUCT, OR HOW PULLMAN IS LIABLE FOR THE ROOFERS' ACTS. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CARLSON BOULEVARD LP dba PULLMAN POINT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM IN PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Andrew Klimenko, Esq. of Dolan Law Firm 421-2800 for pltfs & opposing parties; John C. Ellis, Esq. of Kennick & Associates 714/992-6600 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CARLSON BOULEVARD LP DBA PULLMAN POINT MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM FROM THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege despicable and willful conduct. Plaintiffs have not alleged any intentional torts, or that Pullman was specially aware of the roofers' conduct, or how Pullman is liable for the roofers' acts. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
